result:
**Merge**: Combines branches by creating a new merge commit that ties both histories together. Preserves full history, including the branch structure. Non-destructive.

**Rebase**: Replays your commits on top of another branch, producing a linear history. Rewrites commit hashes. Cleaner log, but destroys the original commit objects.

Key differences:

| | Merge | Rebase |
|---|---|---|
| History | Branched, with merge commit | Linear |
| Commits | Preserved as-is | Rewritten (new SHAs) |
| Conflicts | Resolved once | Resolved per replayed commit |
| Safe on shared branches | Yes | No (force-push required) |

**Rule of thumb**: Rebase local/private work before sharing; merge when integrating public branches.
